name: Iraqi Arabic (Northern / qəltu)
notes: Northern Iraqi 'qəltu' Arabic — the sedentary Mesopotamian type: Mosul (Muslawi), Tikrit, and the Jewish and Christian communal dialects of Baghdad (the latter two now largely diaspora). Named after the reflex of 'I said': qəltu, vs southern gilit (Blanc 1964, §2). MODELLED DELTAS from the gilit parent ar-IQ (cited): (1) DEFINING QƏLTU REFLEX — OA qaf ق → /q/ RETAINED (voiceless uvular): JC/qal/ 'to say' vs Muslim gilit /gal/ (Blanc 1964, §3.26 p.26–27; Jasim 2020, §2.4 — Muslawi Qəltu /q/ is the segment realised as [ɡ] in Baghdadi Gilit). (2) NO KAF AFFRICATION — kaf ك stays /k/; /k/ and /tʃ/ are separate phonemes with NO allophony in Muslawi Qəltu (Jasim 2020, §2.9.2). The inherited gilit affrication rules (IQ_GILIT_AFFRIC_K_*, IQ_GILIT_AFFRIC_G_*) are therefore overridden by id to identity no-ops. (3) INTERDENTALS ث /θ/, ذ /ð/, ظ /ðˤ/ RETAINED — same as gilit, inherited (conservative Mesopotamian feature; Blanc 1964, §3.2; Jasim 2020, §2.4). (4) jim ج → /dʒ/ (retained; inherited). Emphatic backing / tafxim (IQ_EMPH_*) is inherited from ar-IQ and kept — Muslawi Qəltu likewise backs low vowels near emphatics (Jasim 2020, the thesis topic). ENGINE LIMITS documented, not modelled: (a) CHRISTIAN BAGHDADI merges the interdentals to dental stops (θ→t, ð→d, ðˤ→dˤ) and preserves /q/ (Blanc 1964, §3.2; Jasim 2020, §2.3) — this sub-variety split is not orthographically separable from Mosul/Jewish qəltu, which retain the interdentals, so the retained-interdental majority reflex is modelled and the Christian merger is documented only. (b) In Muslawi Qəltu and in Jewish/Christian Baghdadi, OA /r/ is in many instances a uvular/velar [ʁ]~[ɣ] (Blanc 1964, §3.24 — 'M/ras/, JC/ɣas/'; Jasim 2020, §2.4 — [ʁ] variants of /r/); this is lexically conditioned and not predictable from the orthography, so it is documented rather than applied as a blanket rule. More archaic morphology than gilit; closer to Classical paradigms (Blanc 1964, ch.4). ENDANGERED: Mosul Arabic heavily disrupted by the 2014–17 conflict. INPUT CONTRACT: vocalised (harakat) input; short vowels surface only when written. Erwin (1963) describes gilit, not qəltu; NOT OBTAINED for this pass.

sources.author: Blanc, H.
sources.title: Communal Dialects in Baghdad
sources.publisher: Harvard University Press (Harvard Middle Eastern Monographs X)
sources.notes: Read (OCR full text). §3.26 (p.26–27) OA/q/: J and C reflex is /q/ in practically every instance — JC/qal/ 'to say'. §3.24 (p.25) OA/r/: apical in Muslim, but velar spirant /ɣ/ in many JC instances — M/ras/, JC/ɣas/ 'head'. §3.2 interdentals: preserved in Jewish (and Mosul), but replaced by dental stops in Christian Baghdadi. ch.4: qəltu morphology more archaic than gilit.

sources.author: Jasim, M.
sources.title: Tafxiːm in the vowels of Muslawi Qəltu and Baghdadi Gilit dialects of Arabic (PhD thesis)
sources.notes: Read (full PDF). §2.4: Muslawi Qəltu keeps voiceless uvular /q/ (the segment realised as [ɡ] in Baghdadi Gilit); [ʁ] variants of /r/ present in Muslawi Qəltu. §2.9.2: in Muslawi Qəltu /k/ and /tʃ/ are separate phonemes with no allophony (no kaf affrication). §2.3: Christian Baghdadi shifts interdentals to stops.

sources.author: Watson, J.C.E.
sources.title: The Phonology and Morphology of Arabic
sources.notes: Secondary reference for the pan-Arabic inventory (not the source of the qəltu claims above).

sources.author: Holes, C.
sources.title: Modern Arabic: Structures, Functions, and Varieties
sources.notes: Secondary reference (not read for this pass).

allophone_rules.notes: Qəltu override (Jasim 2020, §2.9.2): the gilit kaf affrication does NOT apply — /k/ and /tʃ/ are separate phonemes with no allophony. Overrides the inherited ar-IQ rule of the same id to an identity no-op.
Qəltu override (Jasim 2020, §2.9.2): no kaf affrication after a front vowel either. Identity no-op overriding the inherited ar-IQ rule.
Qəltu override: no gilit qaf-reflex affrication (qaf → /q/, not /ɡ/, in qəltu). Identity no-op overriding the inherited ar-IQ rule.
Qəltu override: no gilit qaf-reflex affrication. Identity no-op overriding the inherited ar-IQ rule.
